utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , mrs . martinez . good to see you today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , dr . gomez .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , i'm here seeing mrs . martinez . she's a 43-year-old female . why are we seeing you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , my arm hurts right here . kind of toward my wrist . this part of my arm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you have pain in your distal radius ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how did that happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i was playing tennis , and when i went to hit , um , i was given a , a backhand , and when i did , i m- totally missed the ball , hit the top of the net but the pole part . and , and it just jarred my arm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and did it swell up at all ? or-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it did . it got a ... it had a little bit of swelling . not a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , did , uh , do you have any numbness in your hand at all ? or any pain when you move your wrist ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit when i move my wrist . um , no numbness in my hand .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have any past medical history of anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , yes . allergic , um , l- i have allergies . and so i take flonase .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any surgeries in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . i actually had a trauma of , um , a stabbing of , um ... i actually fell doing lawn work-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: on my rake .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i was wondering where you were going to go with that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . so , let's take a look at , uh , the x-ray of your arm . hey dragon , let's see the x-ray . okay , looking at your x-ray , i do n't see any fractures , uh , do n't really see any abnormalities at all . it looks essentially normal . great . let me examine you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does it hurt when i press on your arm here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . how about when i bend your arm ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's pretty tender , ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about when i go backwards like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not as much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about when i flex like this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but mostly when i do that type of motion ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . so , um , you have pain following hitting your arm on the net . on your exam , you definitely have some tenderness over your distal radius , um , on your arm . you have pain when i stress especially your thumb , and , and flex your thumb . so , i think what you have is basically just a strain and maybe a contusion to that muscle , from hitting it . um , you certainly do n't have a fracture . i think at this point , we're really going to treat it actually conservatively . we'll have you use ice , um , uh , for the , the pain and swelling . and some anti inflammatory . um , what we'll do is give you some motrin , 800 milligrams three times a day , with food . um , if it does n't get any better in the next week or so , let me know , and we'll take a look at you again .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . hey dragon , go ahead and order the procedures , and the medications as discussed . why do n't you come with me . dragon you can finalize the note .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 43-year-old female presenting for evaluation of right arm pain.
Right arm pain
The patient reports pain in the right arm near the wrist.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain in the distal radius.
The patient confirms pain in the distal radius.
The doctor asks how the injury occurred.
The patient hit her arm on a pole while playing tennis.
The doctor asks if there was any swelling.
The patient reports mild swelling.
The doctor asks about numbness in the hand and pain with wrist movement.
The patient denies numbness but reports pain with wrist movement.
The doctor asks about past medical history.
The patient has allergies and uses Flonase.
X-rays show no fractures or abnormalities.
The doctor asks if there is pain with pressure on the arm.
The patient confirms pain with pressure.
The doctor asks if there is pain with bending the arm.
The patient confirms pain with bending the arm.
The doctor diagnoses a wrist strain and contusion.
The doctor recommends ice and follow-up in one week if symptoms persist.
The doctor prescribes Motrin 800 mg three times a day with food.
